channel.title: Comments for page "3. Mr. Money"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "3. Mr. Money"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: God, if I was Dave I'd be soooo not comfortable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1. He'd throw up whatever needs to be traded, that can fit down his throat.
2. Yes.
3. Jakku does not exist.
4. That's not real money.
5-8. See 4.
9. Can you throw up .jpegs?
10, 11. See 4.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What if it was a bartering society?
What if he went to some island somewhere where they trade shells? Would shells come out?
If he went to Jakku would he throw up quarter portions?
If he saw kids trading marbles for other marbles and collecting them and stuff would he throw up marbles?
If he was in the antebellum south and saw slaves exchanged would he throw up human prisoners?
If he was present while the US and Britain exchanged battleships would he throw up warships?
Can he throw up Monopoly money?
Can he throw up bitcoins?
What if someone made up a currency like mom-bucks and determined them to be a valid medium of exchange in their household? What if mombucks were not made of paper; they were coins, or shells called mombucks, or marbles, or something imaginary?
Could a foundation researcher arbitrarily determine a desired object to be acceptable for exchange and show it to Mr Money in order to begin replicating it? Like having people work out a deal by exchanging a given amount of SCP-500?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This honestly made me sadder than the other Little Mister articles because I actually know people like this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Pretty good story exposition.

Here, have a diamond.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this. Well done.

Minor quibble: Building up to the explanation of how he controls his power is… eh. I'd prefer if that came earlier, and you have the story climax at an emotional point rather than an expository point.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, we've had creepy, and surreal, now sad (and Mr. Lie seems to be a funny one). With any luck, these stories will run the gamut of emotions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: But he's a messed-up kind of guy whom people can only tolerate because he can puke money, that's kinda sad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Will we ever learn the fates of the other Misters? Will they ever get to their destination? Who is this mysterious Mr. Redd character?

==STAY TUNED TOMORROW FOR ANOTHER EXCITING STORY IN THE TALES OF MR. COLLECTOR==

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Iiiiinteresting. Mr. Money seems much less tragic than Mr. Clank and much less scary than Mr. Headless, but I like that we're getting a bit more of the meta-story. Is there going to be a story for each Mister? (excepting the ones already in containment, I assume)
